verse_text: Another parable He spoke to them: 'The kingdom of heaven is like leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal till it was all leavened.'

links.context: The leaven of the Pharisees is their doctrine ([[MAT.16:12]]) and their hypocrisy (Lk 12:1). In Scripture, leaven is used both positively (as in [[MAT.13:33]]) and negatively, as it is here. In either case, leaven symbolizes a force powerful enough (and often subtle enough) to permeate and affect everything around it (see [[1CO.5:6]]-8).

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden
